title: Vivarium Population Spender: Immigration Module
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"71nu1","text":"Description of the steps followed by Vivarium Population Spenser library when running the Immigration module. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d72va","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"af59r","text":"The table used in the Immigration component to determine to total number of migrants into a local authority and to sample the gender, age and ethnicity of the immigrants are found here:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4a1bj","text":"https://github.com/alan-turing-institute/daedalus/blob/develop/persistent_data/Immig_2011_2012_LEEDS2.csv","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":105}],"data":[]},{"key":"4cjqf","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1a6p4","text":"The table used in the Immigration component to assign a MSOA to the new immigrants are found here:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7neqs","text":"https://github.com/alan-turing-institute/daedalus/blob/develop/persistent_data/Immigration_MSOA_M_F.csv","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":103}],"data":[]},{"key":"adiil","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"24C3D5C81AA111EB96E00A58A9FEAC2A","url":"https://github.com/alan-turing-institute/daedalus/blob/develop/persistent_data/Immig_2011_2012_LEEDS2.csv"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"04C883751AA111EB96E00A58A9FEAC2A","url":"https://github.com/alan-turing-institute/daedalus/blob/develop/persistent_data/Immigration_MSOA_M_F.csv"}}}}
steps: Get the total number of immigrants expected for that local authority on a year from a local authority level international migration table.
Divide the number of total immigrants to be assigned by the number of steps existing in that year.
Add the fraction of immigrants to be assigned in one time step to the state table of that local authority.
Assign the new immigrants an MSOA based on their age and gender from rates extracted from table of international migration at the MSOA level.
Assign these new immigrants an ethnicity, age and sex sampling from rates extracted from the local authority level international migration table.
creator: Camila Rangel Smith
